howItWorks.intro: This asphalt tonnage calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.

rules.name: Tonnage
rules.formula: Tons = (Length x Width x Thickness/12 x 145) / 2000
rules.explanation: Hot mix asphalt weighs approximately 145 lbs per cubic foot. Divide by 2000 to convert to tons.

howItWorks.outro: Material prices and building codes vary by region. Get local quotes before purchasing.

faq.q: How thick should a residential driveway asphalt be?
faq.a: A residential driveway typically needs 2-3 inches of asphalt over a 6-8 inch compacted gravel base. Commercial parking lots usually require 3-4 inches. Heavy truck areas may need 4-6 inches.

faq.q: How much does asphalt cost per ton?
faq.a: Hot mix asphalt typically costs $80-$150 per ton for materials only. Installed costs range from $3-$7 per square foot depending on thickness, prep work, and location. Prices vary significantly by region and oil costs.
